faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in East Meadow homes?
faqs.answer: Silverfish thrive in East Meadow due to Long Island's humidity levels and the abundance of older homes with basements, crawl spaces, and moisture-prone areas. They're attracted to damp environments and feed on starches found in books, wallpaper, clothing, and cardboard boxes commonly stored in East Meadow attics and basements.

The timeline depends on infestation severity and moisture conditions in your property. We provide follow-up visits to ensure complete eradication and address any underlying moisture issues.
